cards.title: Trump Admin Authorizes Anthropic Mythos for 100+ US Organizations
cards.body: Over 100 companies and government agencies are now authorized to use Mythos 5, including their non-American employees. This marks a significant expansion of access to Anthropic's latest model.

cards.title: OpenAI Limits GPT-5.6 Rollout Following Government Request
cards.body: OpenAI is restricting GPT-5.6 to select partners after the Trump administration requested a cautious approach. The company stated it doesn't believe government access processes should become the long-term default.

cards.title: OpenAI Hires Uber India Chief for Market Leadership
cards.body: OpenAI has appointed a new leader to spearhead its expansion in India, one of its largest markets outside the US. The move reflects the company's push to expand offices, partnerships, and hiring in the region.

cards.title: OpenAI Launches Jalapeño Custom Inference Chip
cards.body: OpenAI unveiled Jalapeño, a custom inference chip built with Broadcom, challenging Nvidia's AI chip dominance. The move joins efforts by Google, Apple, and SpaceX in building proprietary AI hardware.

cards.title: AI Model Capabilities Now Have Real Political Consequences
cards.body: As AI models advance, their capabilities are beginning to have significant political ramifications. Addressing these consequences will require collective action across the industry.

cards.title: White House Requests OpenAI Slow GPT-5.6 Release
cards.body: The Trump administration asked OpenAI to delay its GPT-5.6 release to select partners due to safety concerns. OpenAI plans a controlled rollout rather than broader public availability.

cards.title: Patronus AI Raises $50M for AI Agent Testing
cards.body: Patronus AI, founded by former Meta researchers, secured $50 million to develop digital worlds that stress-test AI agents. The startup is experiencing strong investor demand for its testing solutions.

cards.title: Claude Gaining Ground Among Paid AI Consumers
cards.body: Anthropic's Claude is increasingly winning over paid consumers, despite ChatGPT's dominant market position. Data shows a notable shift in consumer preference toward Claude among subscription users.

cards.title: General Intuition Raises $320M for Video Game AI Training
cards.body: General Intuition is scaling AI trained on millions of hours of gameplay to develop more human-like agent intuition. The $320 million investment demonstrates confidence in action-based AI learning.

cards.title: Databricks Former AI Chief Targets 1000x Power Reduction
cards.body: Un-0, an image-generation tool from Databricks' former AI chief, demonstrates how to dramatically cut AI's energy consumption. The technology shows promise in replicating conventional AI systems more efficiently.

cards.title: Netris Secures $15M Series A from a16z
cards.body: Netris raised $15 million to accelerate neocloud deployment with network-level software. The platform helps operators reduce time-to-market for AI infrastructure.

cards.title: Adobe Acquires Topaz Labs for Content Enhancement
cards.body: Adobe has acquired Topaz Labs, a maker of image and video enhancement tools. The tools will be integrated across Adobe's suite of applications.

cards.title: Amazon Invests Additional $13B in India AI Infrastructure
cards.body: Amazon announced a fresh $13 billion investment in India's AI infrastructure. The move is part of a global race among tech companies to expand AI capabilities in the region.

cards.title: Europe Pushes Back on Washington's Chip War
cards.body: ASML and European officials are resisting US restrictions on advanced chip equipment exports. The debate centers on access to next-generation chip manufacturing tools.

cards.title: Former Infosys Chief Launches IT Services Challenger
cards.body: Vishal Sikka's new startup, backed by Mayfield and Aramco Ventures, aims to disrupt the IT services industry. The venture brings together veterans from SAP, Infosys, and VianAI.

cards.title: Cerebras Stock Plunges After Earnings Guidance
cards.body: Cerebras' stock dropped following its first earnings report as a public company. The AI chipmaker's narrower-than-expected gross margin outlook spooked investors.

cards.title: Engineering Jobs Prove Resilient Against AI Disruption
cards.body: New data suggests engineering roles are actually the most resilient job category despite AI's expansion. Engineers now make up a larger share of new hires across tech companies.

cards.title: Trump Admin Releases Anthropic Mythos to 100+ US Organizations
cards.body: Over 100 companies and government agencies are authorized to use Mythos 5, including their non-American employees. This marks a significant expansion of access to Anthropic's AI model.

cards.title: OpenAI Limits GPT-5.6 Rollout After Government Request
cards.body: OpenAI restricted GPT-5.6 distribution following a government access request. The company argues such restrictions shouldn't become standard practice.

cards.title: OpenAI Expands India Operations With New Leadership Hire
cards.body: OpenAI hired Uber India's chief to lead expansion efforts in its biggest market outside the US. The move includes expanding offices, partnerships, and hiring.

cards.title: Tech Giants Build Custom AI Chips to Challenge Nvidia
cards.body: OpenAI, Google, Apple, and SpaceX are developing proprietary chips as Nvidia's market dominance faces challenge. OpenAI's Jalapeño chip, built with Broadcom, signals the era of total Nvidia dependence ending.

cards.title: AI Capabilities Now Have Real Political Consequences
cards.body: AI model advancements have progressed to where their capabilities create genuine political impacts. Addressing these consequences requires collective action beyond individual company competition.

cards.title: OpenAI Slows GPT-5.6 Release Over Safety Concerns
cards.body: The Trump administration requested OpenAI slow its new GPT-5.6 model release for safety evaluation. The company will share the model with select partners instead of public release.

cards.title: Patronus AI Raises $50M to Test AI Agents
cards.body: Agent-testing startup Patronus AI closed a $50M funding round from former Meta AI researchers. The company helps stress-test AI agents by building digital worlds.

cards.title: Claude Gains Ground Against ChatGPT Among Paid Users
cards.body: Anthropic's Claude is increasingly winning over consumers willing to pay for AI despite ChatGPT's market dominance. Data shows paid subscriber growth favoring Claude.

cards.title: General Intuition Raises $320M for Gameplay-Trained AI
cards.body: General Intuition secured $320M to scale AI trained on millions of hours of video game footage. The approach aims to develop AI with more human-like intuition using action data.

cards.title: Databricks AI Chief's Un-0 Cuts AI Power Costs 1,000x
cards.body: Un-0, an image-generation tool, demonstrates how to replicate conventional AI systems at dramatically reduced power consumption. The breakthrough could transform AI's energy economics.

cards.title: Netris Raises $15M Series A for AI Infrastructure
cards.body: a16z-backed Netris raised $15M for network switch software accelerating neocloud operations. The platform helps reduce time-to-live for AI infrastructure deployment.

cards.title: Adobe Acquires Topaz Labs for Image/Video Enhancement
cards.body: Adobe completed acquisition of Topaz Labs to integrate its AI-powered image and video enhancement tools. Tools will be integrated across Adobe's application suite.

cards.title: Amazon Invests $13B in India AI Infrastructure
cards.body: Amazon announced a fresh $13B investment in India AI infrastructure expansion. The move reflects the global tech race to scale AI capabilities in emerging markets.

cards.title: Cerebras Stock Drops After Disappointing Earnings Forecast
cards.body: AI chipmaker Cerebras reported lower-than-expected gross margins in its first earnings as public company. The margin outlook scared investors accustomed to stronger projections.

cards.title: AI Hasn't Killed Engineering Jobs—They're Growing
cards.body: New data shows engineering roles comprise a larger share of new hires despite AI disruption fears. Engineers remain among the most resilient job categories in tech.

cards.title: Retail Gets Biggest AI Transformation Behind the Scenes
cards.body: AI is reshaping retail through backend decision-making rather than flashy customer-facing tools. The real impact comes from AI controlling product search, inventory, and personalization.

cards.title: IBM Extends Moore's Law Another Decade With New Chip
cards.body: IBM unveiled a prototype chip with 100 billion transistors in fingernail-sized area, doubling previous density. The design could enable faster, more energy-efficient computers for years.

cards.title: Stripe, Anthropic, OpenAI Back Respiratory Infection Research
cards.body: These tech companies are funding new efforts to develop prevention strategies for common respiratory infections. The collaborative funding approach aims to advance preventive health solutions.

cards.title: Web Data Infrastructure Layer Emerges for AI Training
cards.body: AI enterprises need scalable data access, but much relevant information remains blocked or unstructured. A new infrastructure layer is emerging to unlock enterprise data for AI models.

cards.title: Notion Kills Email App as AI Agents Replace Email
cards.body: Notion discontinued its Skiff-influenced email app after most users switched to AI agents instead. The decision reflects how AI is fundamentally changing communication tool adoption.

cards.title: Anthropic Accuses Alibaba of Largest Claude Clone Attack
cards.body: Anthropic alleges Alibaba conducted the largest known cloning attack against Claude AI model. The company calls for punishment against the alleged unauthorized model replication.
